name: Prestige Reset
description: A reset that trades current progression for a permanent reward, multiplier, unlock, or new progression layer.
design_notes: Prestige resets are explicit bargains. They need a preview of what is lost, what becomes permanent, what multiplier or unlock is gained, and how account_progression_state migrates so players are not surprised by irreversible loss.
design_purpose: Extend long-term progression after a track reaches its practical cap.
Let players convert temporary or completed progress into permanent account growth.
Refresh pacing by restarting parts of the game with new bonuses or goals.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are sacrificing a completed build to return stronger, wiser, or with new possibilities.

parameters.name: prestige_requirement_id
parameters.description: Requirement that must be satisfied before the reset becomes available.

parameters.name: reset_scope_id
parameters.description: Progression scope that will be cleared or reduced by the reset.

parameters.name: prestige_reward_id
parameters.description: Permanent reward granted when the reset commits.

parameters.name: permanent_unlock_flag
parameters.description: Whether the prestige reward survives future resets.

parameters.name: prestige_multiplier_cap
parameters.description: Maximum stacking multiplier or bonus allowed from repeated resets.

edge_cases: A player triggers prestige while pending rewards, purchases, or crafting transactions are unresolved.
Reset scope includes abilities used to access the current area, risking a soft-lock after prestige.
Repeated prestige bonuses hit cap and need a clear explanation of remaining value.
A patch changes prestige_requirement_id for accounts that are already eligible.
common_bugs: Reset clears the reward currency before prestige_reward_id is granted.
Temporary run bonuses are accidentally copied into permanent account_progression_state.
Confirmation preview omits items, currencies, or unlocks that will be lost.
Prestige commit is not atomic, so a crash can apply the reset without granting the permanent reward.
balancing_notes: Prestige rewards should make the next loop feel different, not only numerically faster.
Reset cost must respect player time investment; irreversible resets need unusually clear previews.
Diminishing returns or caps help repeated prestige stay valuable without destroying pacing.
accessibility_notes: Require clear confirmation with a checklist of lost and retained progress.
Allow players to review prestige benefits and reset consequences without a countdown or pressure timer.

implementation_notes.unity: Commit prestige as one transaction: validate prestige_requirement_id, snapshot reset_scope_id state, grant prestige_reward_id, clear scoped progress, save, then refresh UI from committed account state.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep reset previews as pure functions over saved state so confirmation UI matches the actual commit; write permanent rewards before clearing temporary resources.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a progression subsystem or backend profile operation for prestige; authoritative code should reject stale eligibility and persist the reset plus reward atomically.
implementation_notes.web: Represent prestige as a migration-like save operation with before/after state, reset_scope_id, and transaction_id so interrupted commits can resume or roll back safely.

example_games.title: Cookie Clicker
example_games.note: Uses ascension to reset progress for persistent bonuses.

example_games.title: Realm Grinder
example_games.note: Uses resets as a major layer of long-term progression.
